diagrams.title: CNN Architecture for Image Classification
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the architecture of a Convolutional Neural Network used in computer vision, from input image through convolutional and pooling layers (feature extraction) to fully connected layers (classification) and output predictions.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how deep learning models process images through hierarchical feature extraction, transforming raw pixels into abstract representations that enable classification, and demonstrates the core building blocks of all modern computer vision models.

diagrams.title: Object Detection Pipeline
diagrams.description: A diagram illustrating the object detection process showing input image, region proposal or anchor boxes, feature extraction, bounding box regression, and class prediction, comparing one-stage (YOLO) and two-stage (R-CNN) approaches.
elements: Bounding Box Coordinates (x, y, w, h)
Final Detected Objects with Labels and Confidence Scores
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how computer vision models locate and identify multiple objects within a single image, showing the difference between one-stage detectors (fast, real-time) and two-stage detectors (more accurate), which is essential for applications like autonomous driving and surveillance.

diagrams.title: Computer Vision Applications Overview
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the major applications of computer vision in deep learning, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, face recognition, and pose estimation, with example use cases for each.
elements: Object Detection (bounding boxes on multiple objects)
diagrams.purpose: Provides a comprehensive overview of the various computer vision tasks and their real-world applications, helping learners understand the breadth of computer vision and choose appropriate techniques for different problems.
